description:
A conceptual design engineer is modifying a HALE (High-Altitude Long-Endurance) UAV model in OpenVSP. The base model is loaded from /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/hale_uav.vsp3. You must map solar arrays onto the wing and a morphing hinge onto the horizontal tail using OpenVSP's SubSurface feature (found under the 'Sub' tab of the component geometry window).

Read the geometric bounds from the specification file located at /home/ga/Desktop/subsurface_spec.txt. 

1. Add two new SubSurfaces to the 'Wing' component (named Solar_Array_Inboard and Solar_Array_Outboard) and set their Type to Rectangle. Configure their U and W start/end parameters according to the spec.
2. Add one new SubSurface to the 'Horiz_Tail' component (named Morphing_Hinge) and set its Type to Line. Configure its U and W parameters according to the spec.
3. Save the modified model as /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/hale_uav_subsurfaces.vsp3.
4. To validate that the subsurfaces are aerodynamically valid, run the Degen Geom analysis (Analysis > Degen Geom) on the modified model and export the CSV to exactly /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/hale_uav_degengeom.csv.
